01_initial_request.input_data: Protein-ligand complex structural information, amino acid sequences, and public datasets
01_initial_request.user_intent: To facilitate new drug design by rapidly and accurately predicting protein-ligand binding affinity, addressing limitations of traditional scoring functions and existing deep learning methods
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Achieve excellent predictive performance on public datasets

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein-ligand-binding affinity prediction is crucial for drug discovery but experimental methods are expensive. [Gap] Traditional scoring functions are simplistic and existing deep learning methods have limitations in data representation. [Decision] To achieve accurate binding affinity prediction, chose to use PDBbind_v2016 dataset to prepare protein-ligand complex data, expecting to obtain structured training, validation, and test sets.

parameters.data_quality: refined set for validation, core set for testing
parameters.sampling_strategy: randomly select 1000 samples on refined set for validation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Dataset prepared with training set of 11,998, validation set of 1,000, and test set of 285 samples from PDBbind core set

references.citation: Wang R, Fang X, Lu Y et al (2005) The pdbbind database: methodologies and updates. J Med Chem 48(12):4111–4119
references.relevance: Provides the dataset source and methodology for protein-ligand binding affinity data

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein-ligand complexes need to be represented in multiple perspectives for comprehensive feature extraction. [Gap] Previous works used single representation methods losing important structural information. [Decision] To achieve multi-perspective representation, chose to use graph construction method to create ligand molecular graph, protein binding pocket graph, and interactive bipartite graph, expecting to obtain three graph data structures capturing intramolecular and intermolecular forces.

parameters.ligand_graph: G_ligand = {V_ligand, E_ligand}, nodes represent atoms, edges represent chemical bonds
parameters.protein_pocket_graph: G_pocket = {V_pocket, E_pocket}, constructed for protein pocket structure only
parameters.bipartite_graph: G_inter = {V_l, V_p, E}, nodes partitioned into ligand and protein atom sets
parameters.cutoff_distance: distance threshold for bipartite graph edge construction

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Three graph structures created: ligand molecular graph, protein binding pocket graph, and interaction bipartite graph

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Complete protein graphs are computationally expensive due to thousands of atoms. [Gap] Need to capture global protein information without high computational cost. [Decision] To achieve global protein information capture, chose to use amino acid sequence representation to replace complete protein graph, expecting to obtain protein sequence data Seq_protein for global structure information.

parameters.sequence_format: Seq = {a1, a2, a3, ..., ak}, where ai represents amino acid abbreviation
parameters.purpose: capture global information of protein structure without complete protein graph

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein amino acid sequences extracted to represent global protein structure information

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ligand and protein pocket graphs need to be encoded to obtain embedding vectors. [Gap] Need appropriate GNN encoder for homogeneous graph structures. [Decision] To achieve ligand and protein pocket encoding, chose to use AttenFP encoder to perform homogeneous graph neural network encoding on G_ligand and G_pocket, expecting to obtain embedding vectors d_ligand and d_pocket.

parameters.node_update: iteratively updates node information by aggregating neighbor nodes and edges
parameters.pooling: global pooling function to obtain graph feature vector
parameters.initial_features: atomic properties, chemical bond properties from Tables 1 and 2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained ligand graph embedding vector d_ligand and protein pocket graph embedding vector d_pocket

references.citation: Xiong Z, Wang D, Liu X et al (2019) Pushing the boundaries of molecular representation for drug discovery with the graph attention mechanism. J Med Chem 63(16):8749–8760
references.relevance: Provides the AttenFP encoder methodology for molecular graph representation

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Interactive bipartite graph has nodes from different sets with different distributions. [Gap] Homogeneous GNN message passing cannot be directly applied to bipartite graphs. [Decision] To achieve bipartite graph encoding focused on intermolecular forces, chose to use Bipartite Graph Neural Network Encoder to perform edge-level pooling on G_inter, expecting to obtain embedding vector d_inter.

parameters.message_passing: two message passing functions for different node sets
parameters.edge_feature: initial edge feature is one dimensional representing distance between nodes
parameters.pooling_methods: max pooling and weight sum pooling
parameters.output: concatenate m_max and m_w as embedding vector d_inter

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained bipartite graph embedding vector d_inter focused on intermolecular forces

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein sequence contains global information that needs to be encoded. [Gap] Need pretrained model to extract protein sequence embeddings. [Decision] To achieve protein sequence encoding, chose to use ProtBert to perform pretrained protein language model encoding on Seq_protein, expecting to obtain embedding vector d_protein.

parameters.input_format: amino acids as words, complete protein sequence as sentence
parameters.processing: transformer attention stack, output of last hidden layer used
parameters.preprocessing: completed during data preprocessing to reduce memory consumption

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained protein sequence embedding vector d_protein capturing global protein information

references.citation: Elnaggar A, Heinzinger M, Dallago C et al (2020) Prottrans: towards cracking the language of life's code through self-supervised deep learning and high performance computing
references.relevance: Provides the ProtBert pretrained protein language model methodology

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Four embedding vectors from different perspectives need to be combined for final prediction. [Gap] Need to integrate multi-perspective information for affinity prediction. [Decision] To achieve binding affinity prediction, chose to use Multilayer Perceptron to perform concatenation and prediction on d_ligand, d_pocket, d_inter, and d_protein, expecting to obtain predicted binding affinity value y.

parameters.input: concatenated embedding vectors from four perspectives

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Predicted binding affinity values obtained for protein-ligand complexes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model performance needs to be evaluated against baseline methods. [Gap] Need quantitative metrics to assess prediction accuracy. [Decision] To achieve model evaluation, chose to use Error Evaluation Metrics to perform comparison on test set, expecting to obtain MAE, RMSE, SD, R, and Kendall's Rank Correlation values.

parameters.metrics: MAE, RMSE, SD, Pearson correlation coefficient (R), Kendall's Rank Correlation Coefficient (Rk)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MAE reduced by 3.7% relative to best baseline model, Kendall Correlation Coefficient of 0.562

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to verify the effectiveness of different bipartite graph modeling levels. [Gap] Unclear whether atomic or amino acid level modeling is better for interaction. [Decision] To achieve bipartite graph optimization, chose to use three different bipartite graph levels (atom-atom, motif-amino acid, atom-amino acid) to perform comparative analysis, expecting to determine optimal modeling level.

parameters.levels_tested: atom-atom level, motif-amino acid level, atom-amino acid level
parameters.motif_extraction: molecular tree decomposition algorithm to convert ligand molecules into rings, bonds, bridges
parameters.evaluation: loss curves and correlation scatter plots

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Atom-atom level achieved best performance; motif and amino acid node features were simple one-hot vectors

references.citation: Jin W, Barzilay R, Jaakkola T (2018) Junction tree variational autoencoder for molecular graph generation
references.relevance: Provides molecular tree decomposition algorithm for motif extraction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to verify the contribution of each perspective to model performance. [Gap] Importance of multi-perspective information integration needs validation. [Decision] To achieve ablation study, chose to use seven groups of ablation experiments to perform information removal analysis, expecting to demonstrate importance of each perspective.

parameters.ablation_groups: only intermolecular forces, only intramolecular forces, no sequence data, only global information, only ligand information, only protein pocket information, ligand and protein pocket information

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Removing sequence information and interactive bipartite graph decreased performance; optimal cutoff distance is 5 Å

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need to visualize whether different perspective embeddings learned different properties. [Gap] Need to verify heterogeneity of protein and ligand representations. [Decision] To achieve embedding visualization, chose to use t-SNE algorithm to perform dimensionality reduction on embedding vectors, expecting to observe different feature distributions.

parameters.visualizations: protein-ligand complexes (Fig 8A), ligand atoms (Fig 8B), protein atoms (Fig 8C)
parameters.color_coding: green for ligand embeddings, blue for protein pocket embeddings, yellow for bipartite graph embeddings

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ligand and protein pocket embeddings follow different feature distributions; same atoms get different embeddings through different encoders

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparative evaluation on PDBbind_v2016 core set with baseline models and ablation studies

MAE.interpretation: Reduced by 3.7% relative to best baseline model; average of 10-fold cross-validation

Pearson_R.value: good results compared with other methods
Pearson_R.interpretation: Demonstrates good correlation between predicted and experimental values

Kendall_Rk.interpretation: 23% of samples (13 groups) have completely correct sorting; 7% have poor ranking results

optimal_cutoff_distance.interpretation: Model works best when cutoff distance is 5 Å

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The multi-perspective model achieved excellent predictive performance on public datasets (PDBbind_v2016), with MAE reduced by 3.7% compared to best baseline. The model successfully facilitates drug design by rapidly and accurately predicting protein-ligand binding affinity, addressing limitations of traditional scoring functions and existing deep learning methods through multi-perspective data representation.
